title: Documentation, APIs, Licenses, and Generated-Artifact Gates · Gate Defense
summary: Keep human and machine contracts synchronized with implementation. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a documentation and contract drift suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test README setup and core commands from a clean environment.
Validate API schemas, generated clients, migrations, and compatibility changes.
Test documentation links and executable examples where practical.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates docs contracts. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates docs contracts. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for regenerate schemas, clients, docs, and fixtures and fail on unexplained diffs.

content.text: Regenerate schemas, clients, docs, and fixtures and fail on unexplained diffs. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Preserve authority, safety, accessibility, traceability, and stakeholder consequences alongside technical correctness.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates docs contracts. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Generated Drift, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a documentation and contract drift suite.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Generated files can be edited safely by hand. This fails the Generated Drift contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: TypeScript TSConfig Reference (TypeScript 5.9) — Controls strict typing, unused code checks, indexed access, and compiler gate behavior.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Regenerate schemas, clients, docs, and fixtures and fail on unexplained diffs.

stimulus.title: Generated Drift failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Regenerate schemas, clients, docs, and fixtures and fail on unexplained diffs.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Generated Drift applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Regenerate schemas, clients, docs, and fixtures and fail on unexplained diffs.
